content.claim: Disadvantaged children are increasingly concentrated in rural settlements, with up to 94% disadvantage rates in certain rural settlements, driven partly by spatial selectivity, catchment policies, and school closures in peripheral areas.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Finding of systemic causation is interpretive; does not isolate causal weight of school closures versus residential mobility and poverty sorting
No counterfactual comparison to settlements where schools were retained
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
